research.summary: Saras Che is a modern Indian snack brand that reimagines traditional khakhra, bhakhri and ragi-based snacks into convenient, healthier formats with bold global and Indian flavours. Their positioning focuses on whole ingredients, no palm oil, and an easy, mess-free snacking experience, emphasizing both taste and everyday convenience.

palette.role: primary accent — spice red/orange used in highlights, CTAs and key flavour cues
secondary accent — earthy olive green for natural/health cues and badges
background — creamy off‑white for clean layouts and seamless backdrops
text — dark grey for body copy and high-contrast UI text
supporting accent — warm snack/cheese orange used in flavour blocks and props
supporting accent — toasted brown for grain/spice cues and secondary typography

research.aesthetic: Saras Che’s feed mixes packshots on light seamless backdrops with snack-in-use moments and ingredient flat-lays. Backgrounds and surfaces are primarily matte cream/off-white sweeps (#F8F8F8), light wood tabletops, and pale stone/concrete, occasionally swapped for soft colour fields keyed to flavour (warm reds, olives, and oranges) with minimal visible horizon lines. The colour grade is warm and food-forward: slightly boosted saturation in the reds, oranges and greens, neutral-to-warm whites, and gentle contrast so snacks look crisp but still soft and inviting; the overall palette leans to spice reds (#D9534F), earthy greens (#6B8E23), creams and toasted browns. Styling density is low to medium: hero packs and bowls of coin khakhra are kept clean with a few well-chosen props like small katoris of spices, chillies, herb sprigs, grain flour, tea/coffee cups and simple ceramic plates, avoiding clutter and busy linens; recurring props include hands reaching for or holding individual coins, small serving boards, and occasional laptops/books to signal modern snacking moments. Lighting is soft, diffused and directional from one side (window-like daylight or softbox), with short, soft shadows that define texture without harshness; highlights are controlled to keep packaging readable and oil sheen minimal. Compositions favour straightforward front-on packshots and overhead flat-lays, often centred or on rule-of-thirds with consistent negative space; crops alternate between tight close-ups of stacked coins/chips and medium frames showing packs plus bowls. Models appear mainly as anonymous hands and partial torsos interacting with the snacks rather than full portrait-focused shoots, keeping the product as the clear hero while still conveying human usage.
